growth_records.medium: Brackish carbonate-buffered sulfate-reducer medium (Widdel & Bak 1992, without sulfate) + North Sea crude oil
growth_records.cite: Gray et al. The quantitative significance of Syntrophaceae and syntrophic partnerships in methanogenic degradation of crude oil alkanes. Environmental Microbiology (2011). DOI:10.1111/j.1462-2920.2011.02570.x. [PMC3258425]
growth_records.snippet: Calculated specific growth rates were 0.02, 0.01 and 0.01 day-1 respectively... the Methanosarcinaceae and Methanosaetaceae required 69 days. respective growth yields... 2-2.2 x 10-3... 0.031-0.035 g(cell-C)/mol(CH4)
growth_records.notes: Environmental microcosm, mixed community. mu = 0.01 day-1 (qPCR fit R2=0.78) = 0.01/24 = 4.2e-4 /h; doubling time reported 69 days = 69*24 = 1656 h. Growth yields reported 2-2.2e-3 g cell-C/g alkane-C and 0.031-0.035 g cell-C/mol CH4. Temperature/pH not stated.
